lifecycle.operator_decision_summary: Operator 2026-07-02 ordered per-MT drift spot-checks with adversarial sub-agent review; no MT has been validated by an official validator. Wave-B audit: JournalPaneFactory/JournalPanelWidget/JournalPanelState have zero production consumers; the mounted PaneType::LoomDailyJournal is MT-067's thin calendar-interop view (graph/daily_journal_panel.rs:155, app.rs:1150-1164) reusing only DateNav - no journal document load, no RichEditorWidget content area, no 3s auto-save. Core deliverable (open/create today's note and edit it) is unreachable by an operator. CalendarInteropService also has no production constructor; openDailyJournal never called live. Widget-level proofs are real; mount is missing.

scope.summary:
Implement the daily notes / journal surface: a dedicated egui panel that opens or creates today's daily note LoomBlock via the backend openDailyJournal API, renders it as a rich-text document (using the MT-012 renderer), and provides date navigation (prev/next day, calendar picker). This is the native Rust port of app/src/components/LoomDailyJournalPanel.tsx (MT-257).

EXPECTED DIFF SHAPE:
- src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_text/daily_notes/mod.rs
- src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_text/daily_notes/journal_panel.rs (top-level egui panel widget)
- src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_text/daily_notes/date_nav.rs (prev/next day buttons + current date display + calendar popup)
- src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_text/daily_notes/journal_store.rs (async load/save of the daily LoomBlock and its associated RichDocument)

BACKEND FLOW (port LoomDailyJournalPanel.tsx):
1. On mount (or date change): call PUT /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/journals/{journal_date} (openDailyJournal in api.ts). This idempotently creates or retrieves a LoomBlock of content_type = 'journal' for the given date. Returns LoomBlock { block_id, title, content_type='journal', ... }.
2. The LoomBlock for a journal entry may have an associated RichDocument (via the block's linked knowledge document). If the block has a document_ref, load the document via GET /knowledge/documents/{document_id}. If not, show the block title and a 'Start writing' prompt that creates a new RichDocument linked to the block.
3. The loaded RichDocument content is rendered by the MT-012 RichEditorWidget (pass the DocJson from the document's content_json field).
4. On save (Ctrl+S or auto-save on idle): call MT-020 save logic (PUT /knowledge/documents/{id}/save). Do NOT re-call openDailyJournal on save.

PANEL LAYOUT (journal_panel.rs):
- Header: [← prev day] [date display: 'Thursday, June 19, 2026'] [next day →] [calendar icon → opens date picker popup] [today button]
- Subtitle: 'Daily Note' in muted text, block_id badge on hover
- Content area: RichEditorWidget for the journal document (or 'Start writing...' placeholder if no document yet)
- Footer: last-saved timestamp ('Saved 2 min ago') + word count + character count

DATE NAVIGATION (date_nav.rs):
- Prev/next buttons: increment/decrement the current date by 1 day using chrono::NaiveDate arithmetic.
- Today button: jump to today's date (chrono::Local::today().naive_local()).
- Calendar picker: a simple month grid popup (render a 7-column egui grid of day numbers for the current month; click a day to navigate). Highlight today. Highlight the currently displayed date.
- AccessKit: prev = 'journal-prev-day', next = 'journal-next-day', today = 'journal-today', calendar = 'journal-calendar-toggle', date-display = 'journal-date-display'.

STATE MACHINE (journal_store.rs):
- States: Idle, Loading { date: NaiveDate, workspace_id: String }, Ready { date, block: LoomBlock, doc: Option<RichDocLoad>, editor_state: RichEditorState }, Error { date, kind: JournalErrorKind }.
- JournalErrorKind: OpenFailed(String), DocLoadFailed(String), SaveFailed(String).
- Transitions: mount/date_change -> Loading; on openDailyJournal success -> Ready (with doc load if document_ref present); on error -> Error state with typed message.
- Error state renders a typed error chip (not blank) with a 'Retry' button.

AUTO-SAVE:
- 3-second idle timer: after the last keypress, if the doc is dirty (edits since last save), trigger a save. Reset the timer on each keystroke.
- Implement via egui's ctx.request_repaint_after(Duration::from_secs(1)) and tracking last_edit_time in the panel state.

STEP-BY-STEP:
1. In journal_store.rs, define JournalState enum and async fn load_journal(workspace_id, date) -> JournalLoadResult that calls openDailyJournal then optionally loadRichDocument.
2. In date_nav.rs, implement DateNavWidget with the prev/next/today/calendar buttons. Calendar renders a 7-column month grid using egui Grid.
3. In journal_panel.rs, mount date_nav at the top, then match on journal_store state to render: spinner, error chip, or RichEditorWidget.
4. Wire auto-save: track last_edit_time: Option<Instant>; on frame update, if dirty and elapsed > 3s, call the save fn.
5. Footer: compute word count by splitting the full document text on whitespace. Character count = total rope char count across all TextLeafs.
6. Cargo tests: state machine transitions correctly from Loading to Ready. Date navigation: prev from 2026-01-01 goes to 2025-12-31. Calendar grid renders 28-31 day cells. Auto-save timer fires after 3 seconds.
7. AccessKit ids on all controls.

CRATES: chrono already added in MT-017. No new crates required.

DEPENDENCIES: MT-011 (DocModel for journal document content), MT-012 (RichEditorWidget as the content renderer). MT-020 (save logic) is invoked for auto-save; if MT-020 is not yet complete, stub the save call with a log message and mark save as pending in this MT's scope.
acceptance_criteria: Panel opens and displays today's date in the header (kittest, using a fixed mock 'today' = 2026-06-19).
PUT /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/journals/2026-06-19 is called on mount; a mock response returns a LoomBlock (cargo test with mock HTTP).
Prev day button changes the date to 2026-06-18 and triggers a new openDailyJournal call (cargo test).
Next day button from 2026-06-18 returns to 2026-06-19 (cargo test).
Today button from any date returns to 2026-06-19 (cargo test with fixed mock today).
Calendar popup shows a 7-column month grid for June 2026 with correct day numbers (kittest screenshot).
Error state (simulated openDailyJournal failure) shows a typed error chip with a Retry button (kittest).
Word count in the footer matches the number of whitespace-separated tokens in the journal content (cargo test).
Auto-save fires after 3 seconds of idle typing (cargo test using a mock clock advancing time).
AccessKit: 'journal-prev-day', 'journal-next-day', 'journal-today', 'journal-date-display' are present in the kittest accessibility tree.
All cargo tests for daily_notes module pass with 0 failures.
proof_targets: cargo test -p handshake-native -- daily_notes outputs 'test result: ok'.
kittest screenshot saved to src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/screenshots/mt019_journal_panel.png showing header, date, content area, footer.
kittest screenshot saved to src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/screenshots/mt019_calendar_popup.png showing the month grid.

Integration test (real backend): cargo test -p handshake-native --test test_daily_notes -- daily_notes::test_real_open_today --ignored (calls real backend, verifies LoomBlock returned for today's date).
implementation_notes: chrono::Local::today() is deprecated in chrono 0.4.23+; use chrono::Local::now().date_naive() instead.

The panel must handle the edge case where openDailyJournal succeeds but the returned LoomBlock has no linked RichDocument (document_ref = null). In this case, show a 'Start writing' button that calls createRichDocument (POST /knowledge/documents) with a title matching the date string, then links the new document to the block via updateLoomBlock.
Month grid calendar: use chrono::NaiveDate::from_ymd_opt(year, month, 1).unwrap().weekday() to compute the first day's column offset.

The auto-save timer should use egui frame time tracking, not std::time::Instant directly, to remain testable. Store last_edit_frame: u64 and current_frame: u64; diff > 180 frames at 60 fps ~= 3 seconds.

The journal panel is a separate top-level surface from the rich editor document view. It is mounted as a sibling panel in the WP-011 shell's tile layout (reuse WP-011 modules src/frontend/handshake_native/src/split_layout.rs and src/frontend/handshake_native/src/pane_registry.rs for sibling-panel registration), not as a child of RichEditorWidget.
AccessKit ids must be registered through the WP-011 accessibility infrastructure under src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/ (reuse, do not re-implement an accessibility tree).

Cross-WP dependency: this MT depends on WP-KERNEL-011 shell modules (split_layout.rs, pane_registry.rs, accessibility/) for panel mounting and AccessKit registration; those modules are produced by WP-KERNEL-011 and reused here, not created by this MT.

Intra-WP dependencies MT-011 (DocModel) and MT-012 (RichEditorWidget) live under the new WP-012 rich-text module at src/frontend/handshake_native/src/rich_text/; reuse those rather than re-implementing document model or renderer.

NO PERPETUAL SPINNER (KERNEL_BUILDER gate 2026-06-22 - MT-015 spinner-regression lesson): the Loading-state egui::Spinner (AC-8) is correct UX ONLY while a genuine openDailyJournal fetch is in-flight (runtime present + dispatched). Headless/no-runtime must NOT enter a stuck perpetual Loading - only transition Idle->Loading when a runtime actually spawns the fetch (the exact MT-015 backlinks fix); otherwise render a neutral non-animating state. AC-8's spinner kittest + the screenshot tests MUST use harness.step()/run_steps(N) (NOT harness.run(), which loops forever on an animating Spinner) and the process-wide gpu_lock() mutex for wgpu (MT-018 pattern). The journal panel is a SIBLING top-level surface (split_layout/pane_registry), NOT inside rich_editor_widget, so it won't affect the editor bins - but its own test_daily_notes bin must avoid the run()-on-spinner trap.

BACKEND PROOF REALITY (MT-008/014/015/017 pattern): openDailyJournal (PUT /workspaces/{id}/loom/journals/{date}), doc load (GET /knowledge/documents/{id}), createRichDocument (POST), and auto-save bind the REAL backend; gate the live ACs behind the integration feature + NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF if no seeded backend - do NOT fake. The state machine (Loading/Ready/Error transitions), date nav, calendar grid, word/char count, and auto-save debounce-timer are FULLY unit-testable STANDALONE with a mock backend + mock clock. VERIFY the real LoomBlock + openDailyJournal shape in app/src/lib/api.ts (the document_ref/linked-document field name) before binding - the field-existence lesson (don't assume document_ref exists; check).

MT-020 SAVE SEAM + DEBOUNCE (KERNEL_BUILDER gate): auto-save routes through a MOCKABLE save trait/seam - do NOT hard-depend on unfinished MT-020. Implement+test the dirty-tracking + 3s frame-based debounce timer (egui frame counting per note 70, mock-clock testable) + the save-dispatch call against a mock; the real PUT /knowledge/documents/{id}/save binding reuses MT-017's metadata_client save path or is finalized by MT-020. Auto-save debounced: reset on each keystroke, skip+re-arm if a save is in-flight (MC-001). Rapid date-nav: generation counter cancels stale openDailyJournal responses (MC-002, same as MT-015 backlinks generation). 'Start writing' createRichDocument de-bounced via is_creating flag (MC-003).

DATE + PATHS (KERNEL_BUILDER gate): chrono::Local::now().date_naive() (today() deprecated); date nav via NaiveDate::checked_add_days(Days::new(1)) (MC-005: 2026-01-31+1=2026-02-01, leap years); calendar allocates EXACTLY 6 rows regardless of month (MC-004, no reflow). Module root is rich_editor/ NOT rich_text/ (real tree). Screenshots mt019_*.png to the EXTERNAL Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-test/wp-kernel-012-mt-019/ root + assert_no_local_artifact_dir(). ACCESSKIT VARIANT: field-correct nearest accesskit 0.21.1 Role; ACs assert author_ids (journal-prev-day/next-day/today/date-display/...).
ports_from_react: app/src/components/LoomDailyJournalPanel.tsx (full port: state machine Loading/Ready/Error, date navigation prev/next/today, openDailyJournal call, block title display, error handling)
binds_backend_api: GET /knowledge/documents/{document_id} -> RichDocLoad (loadRichDocument; if block has document_ref)
POST /knowledge/documents -> { document, save_receipt_event_id } (createRichDocument; if block has no document_ref and operator clicks 'Start writing')
PUT /knowledge/documents/{document_id}/save -> RichDocSaveResult (via MT-020 save logic, auto-save on idle)

risks: Auto-save fires while the user is mid-sentence at the 3-second mark, creating a save race with a concurrent edit.
openDailyJournal on every date navigation button press causes N rapid API calls if the operator clicks quickly through dates.
The 'Start writing' createRichDocument call fires multiple times if the operator clicks the button while the first request is in-flight.
Calendar month grid overflows its popup bounds for months with 6 calendar rows (e.g. months starting on Saturday).
chrono NaiveDate arithmetic wraps incorrectly around month/year boundaries (e.g. adding 1 day to Jan 31 gives Feb 28/29, not Feb 31).
minimum_controls: Auto-save must be debounced: reset the idle timer on each keystroke; only fire when no edit has occurred for the full 3-second window. If a save is already in-flight, skip the auto-save and re-arm the timer for 3 more seconds after the in-flight save completes.

Date navigation must cancel any pending in-flight openDailyJournal request before issuing the new one; use a generation counter (u64 incremented on each navigation) and ignore responses with a stale generation.
'Start writing' button must be replaced with a spinner while the createRichDocument request is in-flight; use a is_creating: bool flag in JournalState::Ready.

Calendar grid must allocate exactly 6 rows regardless of the month's actual week count (to avoid layout reflowing between months); a kittest screenshot of a 6-row month (e.g. October 2023) must show all cells within the popup Rect.
Date arithmetic must use chrono::NaiveDate::checked_add_days(Days::new(1)); a cargo test asserts 2026-01-31 + 1 = 2026-02-01 and 2026-02-28 + 1 = 2026-03-01.

research_provenance.source: KERNEL_BUILDER gate + MT-015 spinner lesson + MT-008 backend pattern + MT-017 save path
corrections: Loading spinner only with a live fetch (no headless stuck-Loading); spinner tests use step() not run() + gpu_lock
real-backend openDailyJournal/doc proof or NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF; state/date/calendar/count logic unit-testable
auto-save via mockable seam (no hard MT-020 dep); generation-counter date-nav cancel; createRichDocument debounce
chrono date_naive + checked_add_days; 6-row calendar; rich_editor/ path; external screenshots; accesskit variant

implementation_result.implemented_by: KERNEL_BUILDER folded pipeline wf_42fc3d9f (impl + adversarial-review + harden)
commits: ced25e09 fix harden per adversarial review
implementation_result.proofs: AC-1..12 GREEN; 37 lib + 8 integration tests; clippy clean (boxed large Ready variant); journal panel (date nav ◄ Fri Jun 19 2026 ►, calendar, Today, LB-2026-06-19 badge, toolbar, content, '5 words | 25 characters' footer) + calendar popup (June 2026 6x7 grid, day 19 highlighted) visually verified; screenshots EXTERNAL (git ls-files src/**/*.png empty).
implementation_result.disciplines_held: no-perpetual-spinner (headless_open_does_not_enter_perpetual_loading test; Loading only with a live fetch; spinner kittest uses step() not run()); auto-save via mockable save seam (no hard MT-020 dep) + frame-based 3s debounce + generation-counter date-nav cancel; chrono date_naive + checked_add_days; backlinks panel shows the fixed 'Backlinks not loaded.' neutral state.
implementation_result.backend_shape_corrected: VERIFIED CONTRACT CORRECTION: the contract names LoomBlock.document_ref, but the REAL LoomBlock (app/src/lib/api.ts) has document_id: string|null (no document_ref). Agent bound the verified document_id. Field-existence lesson applied again (hsLink/tableHeader/WP009/rename/now document_id).
implementation_result.blocked: PT-4 real openDailyJournal round-trip = NEEDS_MANAGED_RESOURCE_PROOF (gated #[ignore]+integration, real PUT /workspaces/{id}/loom/journals/{date}; not run offline).
